item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No pressure, I know how projects can pile up. Thank you for reading my too long comments and giving them whatever attention you think they deserve. I may sound harsh, maybe, but only want to help the improvement of works.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry, but this need to be rewritten.

I agree with most of your reasonings, and I shall add this to the pile of projects to do in the future. Note that I have a pretty large heap, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Considering the hands on this work, I feel sorry for my vote, but it is a downvote.

The concept is good, but the article makes it too clear that it only exists to give life to that concept, and I feel lacking of any good reason for anything at all that is happening.

For starters, the anomaly is poorly developed, explained or explored at all. We have a rough idea of what it does, a rough idea of its history, and a random list of anomalous stuff living there for reasons, but none of those things is explored enough even to establish the setting well enough.

A lot of groups are working together with little explanation on why they had set aside their differences for this pet project. And aside a "Thaumiel" classification that is poorly justified by a list of random stuff living there, I see no reason to take this as anything else than a pet project.

WWS is working with MC&D. Why? Why would they trust them!? The site is poorly understood, why on Earth would the Foundation accept such an operation happening at a poorly understood SCP!? Or even so boldly classifying it as Thaumiel before gathering enough data. MC&D knows the standards of the other three parties, why would they enter such agreement for a little touristic attraction?

I can't see this as believable, and I want to. I'm fond of WWS and I always leaned for a Foundation a little more open to dialogue, simply because it is practical. But nothing is practical in this scenario, and the tragedy at the end is just announced. Every player had to play out of character, and the focus changes from what is the point of the article time and again as a result.

Now, I can see this working. I can picture the Foundation, MCF and WWS working together, if given a more elaborated context. Either an anomaly the Foundation do have reason to think it is understood enough to be safe to use, or an anomaly it does need MCF somehow to play a part in the containment of. I can see MC&D joining the mix if both they have some greater interest in the place and some way to pressure the other parties. As much as they are the richest group, to say that the Foundations gave in for a natural reserve is stretching too much, by a hell of a lot.

Sorry, but this need to be rewritten.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
-edit : Somebody fixed all the issues I complained about below! Huzzah!

You're welcome <3

item.title: Awesome
item.content:encoded:
Honestly Not The best idea. But I mean I like it cause it's Thaumiel SCP Witch Is Awesome, Mainly Due To every one in Series 5 being new to the site and wanting to write a Keter SCP series 5 is full of em.
This SCP is a good change for once.

Personally it does not appeal to me but Thaumiel Is a good change thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
-edit : Somebody fixed all the issues I complained about below! Huzzah!

—Alright, here's some useful feedback then:

The reason I was uncertain I had figured it out correctly was that I found it too implausible that nobody in any of the three anomalous groups at all showed any indication of suspecting where the light rail system was coming from. That is what broke my suspension of disbelief and made me suspect that I was interpreting things wrong, because it was too heavy on the idiot ball.

I suggest adding some kind of indirect indication that somebody, anybody in any of the three groups has even a teeny tiny little suspicion/hunch that either :

A: The park is making the rail system. Why wouldn't they suspect this if they have already observed the park making new habitats to fit animals that are introduced to the park? It's implausible that nobody has. (unless anti-memes? but that may be overkill)

B: One of those "other two" groups is trying to get away with installing the rail system on the down-low and won't fess up. I like this one better because it can add a sprinkle of tension between the three groups in their correspondence sections.—

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, that pretty much sums it up

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not sure if I understood this correctly, I'm left with the impression that the habitat itself tried to build the light-rail system to modify itself for the human tours, then got pissed and table-flipped via floods when we kept deconstructing their infrastructure to put in our own systems?

"Screw your park designs, it's all going to be underwater now. This is why we can't have nice things."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pfffft, bro, you left in my purple placeholder text for WWS xD

For any complaining about WWS feeling out of character, I’ll fix that soon enough. Maybe today, maybe tomorrow… I dunno. But anyways, glad to have this up, even if it came out half baked. Thanks Roget for carrying this after I started drifting from the site, and I’m very happy to have a WWS nature reserve on the main list.

Also, glad to know that even my brain-barf placeholder text is enough for an article to survive on the site, that’s tickling me silly!

EDIT: Fixed it up. Unfortunately, this new stuff hasn’t been peer reviewed, as it went straight to the site, but it at least is better than what was there. Enjoy the new WWS section!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: When you shake hands with Mickey D's, don't count on getting all your fingers back.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ain't noone messes with nature octopus

Ain't noone

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Wilsons stuff was written by DarkStuff so I can’t speak for it feeling out of character, I haven’t written MCF in awhile but I’ve always written them as having a narrow focus on donations to survive so perhaps their broad interpretation has changed since the last time I wrote about them

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So I feel like there's a lot written here that just kind of meanders for a while. Wilsons's are out of character here (as are MCF to an extent, I feel) and it kind of makes it hard for me to buy into the story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was wondering who the collab was with, I should have seen it coming when I saw WWS.

Good stuff though! Fun to crit, and fun to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a collaboration between DarkStuff and myself, it's been a few months in the making so I hope everyone enjoys it. Thanks to Othello for crit, unclenicolini for formatting help and anyone else who's name I'm forgetting that looked this over as we worked on it.
